headline: China tightens overseas investment rules after blocking Meta-Manus deal
summary: China issued sweeping new rules on Monday tightening control of overseas deals that involve Chinese investors, technology, data and national security, a month after Beijing ordered Meta to ‌unwind its acquisition of AI startup Manus.  The rules, published by the State Council, or cabinet, will take effect ‌from July 1.  One of the most significant articles requires authorisation for exports of restricted Chinese goods, technologies, services or related data.

content.title: China tightens overseas investment rules after blocking Meta-Manus deal
content.summary: China issued sweeping new rules on Monday tightening control of overseas deals that involve Chinese investors, technology, data and national security, a month after Beijing ordered Meta to ‌unwind its acquisition of AI startup Manus.  The rules, published by the State Council, or cabinet, will take effect ‌from July 1.  One of the most significant articles requires authorisation for exports of restricted Chinese goods, technologies, services or related data.

thumbnail.caption: Illustration picture of Manus AI agent app and Meta logo

headline: Meta Faces Digital Services Act Scrutiny On Financial Scam Controls
summary: European consumer groups have filed coordinated complaints under the Digital Services Act against Meta Platforms, alleging insufficient action to curb financial scams on its services. The complaints call for a regulatory investigation and potential penalties from the European Commission, which could affect Meta's operations in a major market. Meta Platforms trades on the NasdaqGS under the ticker NasdaqGS:META, with a recent share price of about $632.51. For investors watching...

content.title: Meta Faces Digital Services Act Scrutiny On Financial Scam Controls
content.summary: European consumer groups have filed coordinated complaints under the Digital Services Act against Meta Platforms, alleging insufficient action to curb financial scams on its services. headline: Interluxe Group Acquires adMixt to Expand Performance Marketing Capabilities
summary: Interluxe Group, a leading luxury marketing agency, has acquired adMixt, a leading performance marketing agency known for its differentiated proprietary technology and expertise across Meta, Google, TikTok, and other digital platforms. The acquisition creates a powerful, integrated, luxury-focused marketing agency, bringing together proprietary audience data with best-in-class strategy, experiential, communications, media, and performance marketing capabilities.

The acquisition creates a powerful, integrated, luxury-focused marketing agency, bringing together proprietary audience data with best-in-class strategy, experiential, communications, media, and performance marketing capabilities.

headline: China expands curbs on foreign deals, tech transfer after Meta-Manus block
summary: China issued sweeping new rules on Monday tightening control of overseas deals that involve Chinese investors, technology, data and national security, a month after Beijing ordered Meta to unwind its acquisition of AI startup Manus.  The regulations, published by the State Council, or cabinet, seek to ‌influence deals in markets beyond mainland China, including Taiwan, and give Beijing the power to punish foreign firms whose home countries restrict Chinese investment.

content.title: China expands curbs on foreign deals, tech transfer after Meta-Manus block
content.summary: China issued sweeping new rules on Monday tightening control of overseas deals that involve Chinese investors, technology, data and national security, a month after Beijing ordered Meta to unwind its acquisition of AI startup Manus.  The regulations, published by the State Council, or cabinet, seek to ‌influence deals in markets beyond mainland China, including Taiwan, and give Beijing the power to punish foreign firms whose home countries restrict Chinese investment.

headline: Why Google Stock Outshines Amazon
summary: Amazon (AMZN) commands a persistent premium valuation from the broader market, trading at roughly 32x based on its current market price and TTM EPS. That is great for Amazon. But wait a minute, Alphabet (GOOG) currently sits at a noticeably more attractive 29x multiple. Hard to believe, but the market prices Amazon at a persistent premium to Google.
